hero.title: Every span, verdict and dollar, kept on your machine
hero.sub: A single sub-megabyte binary that records what your agent runs actually did — every model call, tool invocation, retry and eval verdict — as plain JSONL files on your own disk. Query latency, errors, token usage and cost per run over a JSON HTTP API or the CLI; a companion dashboard, NullHub, renders the UI on top.

alts.label: Build from source (Zig 0.16.0)
alts.code:
git clone https://github.com/nullclaw/nullwatch
cd nullwatch
zig build
zig build run -- serve

quickstart:
Seed a deterministic demo dataset — no API keys, no running agent — then read it back.

```bash
# seed demo runs: a passing review, a failed tool call, a retry
zig build run -- demo-seed

# start the JSON HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:7710
zig build run -- serve

# list runs and inspect the failure
zig build run -- runs --limit 20
zig build run -- run demo-tool-failure

# same data over HTTP
curl http://127.0.0.1:7710/v1/runs?limit=20
```

features.title: Span ingest for agents
features.body: Records each timed unit inside a run — model calls, tool invocations, memory lookups, retries — with tokens, cost and status attached.

features.title: Evals as first-class data
features.body: An eval is a scored quality check attached to a run — scorer name, score, pass/fail verdict, dataset. Send them over /v1/evals or the CLI and they are stored and queried right next to the trace they judged.

features.title: Run-level summaries
features.body: Each run gets computed totals: duration, error count, token usage, cost, pass/fail counts and an overall verdict.

features.title: A tiny OTLP sink
features.body: Accepts standard OpenTelemetry traces (OTLP/HTTP JSON) on /v1/traces. Point any tool that emits OpenTelemetry traces at it — including NullClaw, the companion agent runtime — and incoming spans are mapped into runs automatically.

features.title: Local JSONL storage
features.body: Everything lands under ~/.nullwatch/data as plain JSONL. Storage is designed to swap for SQLite later without changing the API contract.

features.title: Demo data built in
features.body: Run demo-seed to get a realistic sample dataset — a passing code-review run, a failed tool call, a handoff with retries — with no API keys, no hosted service and no agent running. Re-running it is safe: it always produces the same data.

note: Pre-1.0 with one release so far; the README calls it a bootstrap implementation, and the repo has no license file yet.

plain.what: NullWatch is an observability service in a single sub-megabyte Zig binary: it serves a JSON HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:7710 and stores AI-agent execution traces and eval results as JSONL files under ~/.nullwatch/data.
plain.why: When an agent run fails or burns money, you need a record of every model call, tool invocation and eval verdict to see what happened — NullWatch keeps that record on your own machine instead of a hosted observability platform.
plain.when: Reach for it when an agent run misbehaves and you want to list its spans, find the failed tool call and total up tokens and cost — or when you need a local OpenTelemetry (OTLP/HTTP JSON) endpoint to point an agent's trace output at.
